# Law Office of Cedric P. Collins, LLC

> A divorce, child custody, and family law practice serving Columbus, Pickerington, and Central Ohio, led by attorney Cedric P. Collins.

## About

The Law Office of Cedric P. Collins, LLC helps families in Central Ohio through divorce, child custody, and parenting time matters with honesty, patience, and a clear plan. Attorney Cedric P. Collins has practiced Ohio law since 2012 and is appointed by the courts as a Guardian ad Litem in Franklin, Fairfield, and Licking Counties, which gives him direct insight into how Ohio judges weigh decisions about children.

## Services

### Divorce Representation
Guidance through every stage of an Ohio divorce or dissolution, from filing to final decree, with a focus on protecting your interests and your children. For people in the Columbus and Central Ohio area facing the end of a marriage.
URL: https://www.cedriccollinslaw.com/

### Child Custody and Parenting Time
Representation in custody, parenting time, and decisions made in the best interest of your children. Informed by his work as a court-appointed Guardian ad Litem.
URL: https://www.cedriccollinslaw.com/

### Family and Juvenile Law
Broader family and juvenile law matters for Central Ohio families, including related domestic relations issues.
URL: https://www.cedriccollinslaw.com/

## Ideal Client
Individuals and parents in Columbus, Pickerington, and the surrounding Franklin, Fairfield, and Licking County areas who are going through a divorce or a custody dispute and want a steady, honest advocate.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Do you offer a free consultation for divorce cases?
Yes. The first conversation is free and completely confidential. You will leave knowing where you stand and what your options are, whether or not you decide to work together.

### What areas of Ohio does the firm serve?
The office is in Pickerington and serves the greater Columbus area, including Franklin, Fairfield, and Licking Counties.

### How much does a divorce attorney cost in Columbus, Ohio?
Cost depends on whether the case is contested and how complex the issues are. The first consultation is free and includes an honest picture of likely costs.

### How long does a divorce take in Ohio?
An uncontested dissolution can often be finalized within a few months. A contested divorce involving custody or property disputes takes longer.

### Does the firm handle child custody and parenting time?
Yes. Custody, parenting time, and children's best interests are a core focus, informed by attorney Collins's work as a court-appointed Guardian ad Litem.
